# SlideKit Replication Learnings

## Browser Rendering Consistency

Playwright (headless Chromium) and the user's browser (e.g., Chrome, Firefox, Edge) can produce slightly different visual output for the same slide, even with identical CSS. Differences are subtle — a word that fits on one line in one browser may wrap in another, causing cascading layout differences (tighter spacing, overlapping text, different element heights).

This has a practical consequence for the review workflow:

**The user must review the screenshots generated by the agent, not the live browser.** The scene model metadata (bounding boxes, collisions, warnings) corresponds to the Playwright render, not the user's browser. If the user gives feedback based on what they see in their own browser, the agent may not be able to reproduce or detect the issue because:

1. The screenshot the agent sees may look fine (no overlap, no wrapping)
2. The scene model reflects the Playwright render, so it won't report a collision that only exists in the user's browser
3. The agent will correctly report "not found" for an issue that genuinely exists in the user's browser but not in Playwright

**Workflow rule:** After rendering slides, the agent should generate screenshots and share them with the user. The user inspects those screenshots (not the live browser) and provides feedback. This ensures the agent and user are looking at the same thing, and that any issues flagged by the user are reproducible in the environment where the agent can detect and fix them.
